channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Leading US Democrats withdraw support for Platner after assault allegations
item.description: Monday's accusations are the latest in a long string of controversies surrounding the Maine Democratic Senate candidate.

item.title: All to know about Ronaldo’s World Cup career as it ends in Spain loss
item.description: The Portuguese captain became the only player to score in six different World Cups after his brace against Uzbekistan.

item.title: Late Spain goal eliminates Portugal, ends Ronaldo’s international career?
item.description: Sub Mikel Merino scores in stoppage time to give Spain a 1-0 win over Portugal in the last 16.

item.title: Canada picks Germany’s TKMS for new submarines
item.description: The German-owned naval shipbuilder made the bid with NATO ally Norway, beating out South Korea's Hanwha Ocean.

item.title: ‘If you win, it’s a stain’: Americans react to FIFA-Balogun controversy
item.description: USA supporters divided on Trump's intervention and FIFA's decision to suspend striker's ban in Belgium World Cup match.

item.title: One Phone Call, One Vanishing Red Card — The Balogun Scandal
item.description: Al Jazeera's Basel Ghazoghli (@baselgazi) breaks down how the White House helped Folarin Balogun stay in the World Cup.

item.title: Morocco says it has dismantled ISIL-linked cell planning attack
item.description: Police arrest 10 suspects, seizing bladed weapons, military-style clothes and bomb‑making instructions.

item.title: Influencers say they’re ‘honoured’ to attend Khamenei funeral
item.description: Several social media influencers travelled to Iran for the state funeral of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei.

item.title: Survivors and bodies still being pulled from rubble days after twin quakes
item.description: International aid teams have played a key part in Venezuela’s ‘miracle rescues’, days after twin earthquakes struck.

item.title: Macron arrives in Syria for talks ahead of NATO summit in Turkiye
item.description: The French president is the first leader from western Europe to visit Syria since the overthrow of Bashar al-Assad.

item.title: Mbappe, FFF hit back at Paraguayan senator Amarilla over racist tirade
item.description: Amarilla called Mbappe a 'colonised Cameroonian' after his penalty helped France knock Paraguay out of the World Cup.

item.title: LIVE: USA vs Belgium – FIFA World Cup 2026 round of 16
item.description: Live coverage and text updates of USA against Belgium in last-16 game, overshadowed by Balogun suspension row.

item.title: US judge tosses out Musk’s bid to void Twitter fraud verdict
item.description: Jurors had found owner Elon Musk liable for tweets questioning whether Twitter was overrun by fake and spam accounts.

item.title: Crowds hold ‘destroy Israel’ banner at Spain’s Pamplona bull run
item.description: A giant banner reading ‘Destroy Israel’ was unfurled as thousands gathered for the San Fermin bull run festival.

item.title: Prosecutors in US lay out case against alleged killer of Charlie Kirk
item.description: Judge to decide whether there's enough evidence to bring Tyler Robinson to trial for conservative activist's killing.

item.title: What is the fallout of the increasing violence in Mali?
item.description: Coalition of rebels and armed groups carries out another series of attacks.

item.title: US Supreme Court clears path for Texas to enforce app age verification law
item.description: Parents must approve app downloads for minors as legal fight over Texas age verification law continues.

item.title: Body of Iran’s slain Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei arrives in Qom
item.description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s body arrives in Qom as millions of mourners honour his leadership over past three days.

item.title: NATO urges members to deliver plans for reaching defence spending targets
item.description: US warns of consequences for NATO members without clear spending plans, signalling high expectations from allies.

item.title: Pro-Palestine protesters rally for ‘Filton 25’ activists at UK court
item.description: Pro-Palestine protesters rally for 'Filton 25’ activists at UK court.

item.title: NATO chief calls for ‘credible plans’ to reach defence spending goals
item.description: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte said members are now spending around 4% of GDP on defence.

item.title: Nigeria sees no sign that anti-immigrant violence is waning in South Africa
item.description: South Africa's government has been accused of not doing enough to tackle xenophobic attacks.

item.title: Left-wing candidate Roberto Sanchez concedes Peru vote to Keiko Fujimori
item.description: Announcement comes days after Peru's electoral agency certified right-wing Fujimori as winner in razor-thin race.

item.title: Ebola death toll in DR Congo surpasses 500
item.description: Nation’s 17th Ebola epidemic is fuelled by the Bundibugyo virus, with no existing vaccine or approved treatment options.

item.title: Trump rings in new ‘Trump Account’ investment accounts for children
item.description: US President Donald Trump rang the opening bells of the New York Stock Exchange and Nasdaq to welcome 'Trump Accounts'.
